free_text: Measurement of the neutron-capture cross section of
76Ge and 74Ge below 15MeV and its relevance to
neutrinoless double beta decay searches of 76Ge
Preliminary results.
Irradiations were carried out in the
10-MV FN Tandem Van de Graaff Accelerator at the
Triangle Universities Nuclear Laboratory
A metallic germanium slab 10mm x 10mm in area and
with a thickness of 2 mm (resulting in a mass of
about 1.5 g) with the same isotopic composition as
that of the enriched high-purity germanium detectors
was used as target. The germanium slab was sandwiched
between either indium or gold foils of the same area
and thickness of 0.125mm and 0.01mm, respectively.
detector material with isotopic abundance of
approximately 86% 76Ge and 14% 74Ge.The isotopic
composition is the same as that of the enriched HPGe
detectors used by the GERDA and Majorana
Collaborations.
Sample counting statistics
Monitor statistical uncertainty
Reference cross section
Sample detector efficiency
Monitor detector efficiency
Source geometry and g-ray selfabsorption
of the Ge sample
Source geometry and g-ray selfabsorption
of the monitor
Monitor half-life
Sample half-life
Sample gamma ray intensity
Monitor gamma ray intensity
Monitor neutron flux fluctuation
Sample neutron flux fluctuation
Sample low energy neutron contribution
Monitor low energy neutron contribution
Total uncertainty. Total uncertainty was
obtained by taking the square root of the sum of
the squares of the individual uncertainties.
Compiled by S.H.

The 3H(p,n)3He reaction was used to produce
quasimonoenergetic neutrons between 0.4 and 3.39 MeV
using a tritiated titanium target.
cell. The gas pressure was adjusted to 4 atm to
provide the desired neutron energy spread in the
energy range investigated. Typically the neutron
energy spread was +-150 keV at 0 deg.

The 3H(d,n)4He reaction (Q = 17.59 MeV) was
employed for En=14.8 MeV, using tritiated target
assembly. It consisted of a 2-mg/cm2-thick titanium
layer loaded with 2.5 Ci of  tritium and evaporated
onto a 0.4-mm-thick copper disk

from the relation
sig(g) = "apparent" total cs - 0.19 * sig(m)
(p.152, bottom of left column)
